info.title: Metricool API
info.description:
Metricool API

The base URL for all endpoints is: https://app.metricool.com/api

All endpoints require authentication. To identify yourself properly, you must include 3 parameters in all calls

You can add the parameters directly in the request (the only parameter that can be implemented in the header with name X-Mc-Auth is the userToken)

  • userToken: It is a unique authorization code for each user and can be found in the account settings.
  • userId: the user identifier of your Metricool account.
  • blogId: It is the identification number of the brand, this number can be easily found from the browser url

post.summary: Subscribe to TikTok comment webhook events
post.description: Subscribes to the COMMENT event type on the TikTok Business API. This is an app-level subscription that activates for all users with the comment.list scope. Callback URL: https://app.metricool.com/api/webhooks/tiktokbusiness/catch-all. If a callback URL is already configured, the subscription is skipped.

200.description: Subscription created, updated, or already active

get.summary: Returns a time series for a concrete metric during a period of time

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description:
metric Supported values: Metrics are case insensitive
  • For TikTokAds: impressions, cost, clicks, conversions, cpm, cpc, ctr
  • For TikTok Videos: videos, views, comments, shares, interactions, likes, reach, engagement, impressionSources, averageVideoViews
  • For TikTok Account: video_views, profile_views, followers_count, followers_delta_count, likes, comments, shares
  • For Pinterest: impression save pin_click outbound_click video_mrc_view video_avg_watch_time video_v50_watch_time quartile_95_percent_view pins
  • For Pinterest (accounts): followers, following, delta followers
  • For Youtube: views, interactions, likes, dislikes, comments, shares
  • For Facebook (metrics for stories): storiesCount
  • For Facebook (metrics for posts): count, interactions, engagement, impressionsunique, impressions, clicks, comments, shares, reactions
  • For Facebook (metrics for reels): blue_reels_play_count, post_impressions_unique, post_video_likes_by_reaction_type, post_video_social_actions, engagement, count
  • For Facebook (account metrics): page_posts_impressions, page_actions_post_reactions_total, postsCount, postsInteractions, likes, pageFollows, pageImpressions, pageImpressions.M, pageImpressions.F, pageImpressions.U, pageImpressions.13-17, pageImpressions.18-24, pageImpressions.25-34, pageImpressions.35-44, pageImpressions.45-54, pageImpressions.55-64, pageImpressions.65+, pageViews, page_daily_follows_unique, page_daily_unfollows_unique
  • For GMB: business_impressions_maps, business_impressions_search, business_impressions_total, business_direction_requests, call_clicks, website_clicks, clicks_total, business_conversations, business_bookings, business_food_orders, business_actions_total
  • For Instagram (account metrics): email_contacts, get_directions_clicks, phone_call_clicks, text_message_clicks, clicks_total, postsCount, postsInteractions
  • For Instagram (posts metrics): count, interactions, engagement, reach, impressions, likes, comments, saves, shares
  • For Instagram (reels metrics): count, comments, likes, saved, shares, engagement, impressions, reach, interactions, videoviews
  • For Instagram (breakdown metrics, correct subject and scope are needed): comments, likes, profile_links_taps, reach, saved, saves, shares, total_interactions, views
  • For Linkedin (account metrics [company]): followers, paidFollowers, companyImpressions, deltaFollowers, impressionCount, shareCount, clickCount, likeCount, commentCount, postsCount
  • For Linkedin (posts metrics [company]): posts, clicks, likes, comments, shares, engagement, impressions, interactions
  • For Linkedin (newsletters metrics [company]): posts, clicks, likes, comments, shares, engagement, impressions, interactions
  • For Linkedin (account metrics [personal]): followers, deltaFollowers, reaction, comment, reshare, impression
  • For Linkedin (posts metrics [personal]): count, comment, reshare, impression, reaction, engagement, interactions
  • For Threads (posts metrics): count, views, likes, replies, reposts, engagement, quotes, interactions, shares
  • For Bluesky (posts metrics): posts_count, interactions, likes, replies, reposts, quotes

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: From Date and time ISO 8601 format. e.g. 2021-01-01T10:00:00+01:00

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: From Date and time in ISO 8601 format. e.g. 2021-09-10T03:30:00+01:00

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: (Optional) The timezone used for the date range to search. ('Europe/Madrid')

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Is a filter when we retrieve data. Some examples: 'posts', 'reels', 'account'. Each network defines its own 'subjects'. For breakdowns the possible subjects are: media_product_type, follow_type and contact_button_type

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Is a second filter. Some examples: 'viewed', 'published'. It is used for Youtube and for Instagram metrics breakdown, for the breakdowns the possible values are: BOOK_NOW, CALL, DIRECTION, EMAIL, INSTANT_EXPERIENCE, TEXT, UNDEFINED, FOLLOWER, NON_FOLLOWER, UNKNOWN, AD, CAROUSEL_CONTAINER, POST, REEL, STORY

get.summary: This method is deprecated
Get upload transaction information
get.description: Retrieves metadata about a file that has been uploaded to S3.

put.description: Creates a new upload transaction. Specify resourceType (e.g. PLANNER) and the list of parts. The backend derives the storage bucket and path from the resource type and your user ID. Single-part → simple presigned URL. Multi-part → multipart upload with per-part presigned URLs.

400.description: Invalid request parameters or part configuration

delete.summary: Abort/cancel upload transaction
delete.description: Aborts an upload transaction and cleans up any uploaded parts.

patch.description: Completes an upload transaction. For multipart: provide uploadId, key and parts. For simple: provide the fileUrl of the already-uploaded file. PLANNER resources are automatically moved/converted to metricool-download on completion.

post.summary: Request access to a brand review portal
post.description: Public. Resolves the portal by its permanent publicId and, if the reviewer has posts pending review, emails a fresh tokenized link to the address on file (subject to a resend cooldown). Returns 202 with result=TOKEN_REISSUED when a link is in the inbox, or 200 with result=NO_PENDING_REVIEW when there is nothing to review.

post.summary: Resend a reviewer's portal link (internal)
post.description: Authenticated. For a Metricool user with approve-posts permission on the brand: resends the tokenized portal link to the reviewer's email, if there are pending posts and the 5-minute cooldown has elapsed. Returns 200 with result=SENT, NO_PENDING_REVIEW, or COOLDOWN_ACTIVE (with secondsUntilNextResend).

get.summary: List the posts a brand reviewer must review
get.description: Authenticated (brand reviewer session). Returns the scheduled posts still pending this reviewer's review for the brand, as light rows with each post's state. The {publicId} must belong to the authenticated session.

get.summary: Preview one of a brand reviewer's posts to review
get.description: Authenticated (brand reviewer session). Returns the full detail of a single post the reviewer must review, for the preview modal. The post must be in the reviewer's pending set, else 404. Read-only.

resourceType.description: Type of resource being uploaded. Determines the storage strategy (bucket and path). Case-insensitive. Supported values: 'planner', 'studio-logo'.

parts.description: List of parts that compose the file. Each part includes size, byte range, and verification hash. Parts must be consecutive without gaps, and the total must cover the entire file. A single-part list results in a simple upload. Multiple parts trigger multipart upload.

fileExtension.description: Optional file extension hint (e.g., 'mp4', 'jpg'). If not provided, derived from contentType.

S3PresignedUploadRequest.description: Request to generate presigned URLs for direct S3 upload

size.description: Size of this part in bytes

startByte.description: Starting byte position in the original file (0-based, inclusive)

endByte.description: Ending byte position in the original file (0-based, exclusive)

hash.description: Base64-encoded SHA-256 hash of this part's content. Used for integrity verification with S3 Object Lock.

S3UploadPart.description: Represents a single part of a file to be uploaded with size, byte range, and verification hash

presignedUrl.description: Presigned URL to upload this part

partSize.description: Size of this part in bytes

startByte.description: Start byte offset of this part in the original file

endByte.description: End byte offset of this part in the original file (exclusive)

S3PartPresignedUrl.description: Presigned URL for a single part of a multipart upload

key.description: S3 object key where the file will be stored

expiresAt.description: Expiration time of the presigned URLs

fileUrl.description: Final file URL (available after upload completes)

presignedUrl.description: Presigned URL for simple upload (only for files < 5MB)

uploadId.description: Upload ID for multipart upload (only for files >= 5MB)

parts.description: List of parts with presigned URLs for multipart upload (only for files >= 5MB)

S3UploadResponse.description: Response containing presigned URLs for S3 upload (simple or multipart)

draft.description: Number of DRAFT post entries (one per post-network combination)

rejected.description: Number of rejected post entries (one per post-network combination)

PerformanceDashboardPlannedContentCountD.description: DTO with the count of scheduled post entries linked to a performance dashboard, broken down by status

BrandDto.description: Brand information associated to a Studio report

taskIndex.description: Zero-based position of the failed agent within the version's task list. The first agent is 0.

agentName.description: Name of the agent that failed

errorType.description: Machine-readable failure classification of this agent. Resolved leniently upstream, so it is always one of the allowed values.

FailedAgentDto.description: Detail of a single agent that failed while generating a Studio report version.

content.description: Report content with charts and analysis if status is COMPLETED

errorMessage.description: Error message if status is FAILED

errorType.description: Machine-readable failure classification, present only when status is FAILED. Lets the UI offer the right recovery action.

prompt.description: User prompt to generate the report

studioId.description: Report identifier that contains all the report versions

type.description: Type of the report between Calendar and Analytics

accessBlockedReason.description: Reason why the report is blocked, null when accessible. BRAND_DELETED: brand no longer exists, NO_PERMISSION: user lost FULL_ACCESS_STUDIO, DIFFERENT_OWNER: brand belongs to a different owner than the current brand context

totalVersions.description: Total number of non-deleted versions for the report. Only present when the caller requested includeTotalVersions=true.

customLogoUrl.description: Public URL of the custom logo shown in the anonymous viewer header. Always populated: the user's custom logo URL when set, or the default Metricool logo otherwise.

sharedLink.description: Public shared URL of the report when an anonymous access link is active. Omitted when no link was ever generated or it has been disabled.

selected.description: True for the active (last-successful) version. Only present in the versions list.

hasManualEdits.description: True when the AI-generated elements of this version have been manually edited by its owner. Lets the UI warn that manual edits will not be preserved if the view is regenerated/refreshed.

totalAgents.description: Total number of agents that took part in generating this version. Absent (not present) for versions created before partial-results support — absence means "no agent info", NOT zero.

failedAgents.description: Number of agents that failed while generating this version. Present together with totalAgents for new versions (0 when all agents succeeded); absent for legacy versions. Lets the UI render the partial-success case (Caso 3): some charts generated, some agents failed.

failedAgentDetails.description: Detail of the agents that failed. Only present in the version detail (single-version endpoints); listing endpoints carry just the counters. Absent when there is no agent detail (e.g. legacy versions).

shownVersionState.description: Whether the displayed report version is the latest or a fallback to an older working version, and why. Only present in the reports listing; absent in single-version endpoints. LATEST: the shown version is the report's most recent one. FALLBACK_WHILE_RUNNING: the most recent version is still running/enqueued, so the last working version is shown while it regenerates. FALLBACK_AFTER_FAILURE: the most recent version failed, so the last working version is shown instead.

prompt.description: User prompt for the report generation
prompt.example: Quiero un gráfico de barras que compare mis estadísticas de tiktok de todas mis marcas

customLogoUrl.description: Public URL of a custom logo to display in the anonymous viewer header. Must be an HTTPS URL hosted on a Metricool S3/CDN domain. If null, the default Metricool logo is used.

audioId.description: Unique identifier of the audio asset from the Instagram Audio API.

igUsername.description: Instagram username of the creator (populated for 'original_sound' type).

coverArtworkUrl.description: URL of the cover artwork thumbnail.

durationMs.description: Duration of the audio in milliseconds.

audioVolume.description: Volume level for the audio asset, from 0 (muted) to 100 (full volume). Defaults to 100.

videoVolume.description: Volume level for the video's original audio, from 0 (muted) to 100 (full volume). Defaults to 100.

allowedCountryCodes.description: A string list of valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes that represents the countries where this media should be shown.

items.description: A string list of valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes that represents the countries where this media should be shown.

isSpoiler.description: A boolean that represents if isSpoiler is activated or not

replyControl.description: A string that represents who can reply to a Threads post, been EVERYONE by default. (EVERYONE, ACCOUNTS_YOU_FOLLOW,MENTIONED_ONLY,PARENT_POST_AUTHOR_ONLY,FOLLOWERS_ONLY)

shareAsInstagramStory.description: A boolean that represents if the Threads post must be cross-shared as an Instagram Story. The Story is published on the Instagram account linked to the Threads account, which may not match the Instagram account connected to this brand.

instagramStoryTheme.description: Theme used when the Threads post is cross-shared as an Instagram Story (LIGHT or DARK). Only allowed when shareAsInstagramStory is enabled; must be absent otherwise.

ScheduledPostThreadsData.description: Data needed for schedule a Threads post

result.description: Outcome of the access request. TOKEN_REISSUED: a fresh link is in the reviewer's inbox (sent now, or recently within the resend cooldown). NO_PENDING_REVIEW: nothing pending to review, no email sent (the portal shows its neutral empty state).

Outcome. SENT: a fresh link was emailed to the reviewer now. NO_PENDING_REVIEW: nothing pending, no email sent. COOLDOWN_ACTIVE: blocked by the 5-minute resend cooldown, retry after secondsUntilNextResend.

secondsUntilNextResend.description: Seconds until a resend is allowed again. Present only when result=COOLDOWN_ACTIVE.

BrandLite.description: Brands the user will lose after the downgrade

disconnectPreviousConnections.description: Flag to confirm duplicate as primary

maxRecoveryDays.description: Max recovery days for the deleted brand (max 45 days)

agencyId.description: Brand agency id (if the brand belongs to an agency)

isOwner.description: Indicates if the user is the owner of the agency

teamMemberRoleId.description: Unique identifier of the team member role assigned to the user

agencyOwnerUserId.description: Unique identifier of the agency owner user

keptBrands.description: Brands the user will keep after the downgrade

lostBrands.description: Brands the user will lose after the downgrade

SubscriptionDowngradeBrandsLostCompariso.description: Brands comparison result for a subscription downgrade

enabled.description: Effective enabled state after applying the per-cell override, the global email opt-out and the event default

NotificationChannelPreferenceDto.description: Effective enabled state of a notification event for a single channel

NotificationEventPreferenceDto.description: Resolved notification preferences for a single event, across all channels

events.description: Resolved matrix, one entry per event

emailGloballyOff.description: Global email opt-out flag; when true, every EMAIL channel is effectively disabled

NotificationPreferencesDto.description: Full resolved notification preferences matrix for the authenticated user

UpdateNotificationPreferenceRequest.description: A single granular change to the notification preferences matrix

UpdateNotificationPreferencesRequest.description: Batch of granular changes to apply to the notification preferences matrix

emailGloballyOff.description: When true, all EMAIL channel notifications are disabled regardless of per-event overrides

UpdateEmailGloballyOffRequest.description: Request to toggle the global email opt-out flag
